questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Watch for deer crossing the road
Never drive in this lane
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Come to a complete stop
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Come to a complete stop
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
U-turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Left turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Right turns are not allowed
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Left turns are not allowed
U-turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Right turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Right turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
U-turns are not allowed
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Start of a no-passing zone
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Start of a no-passing zone
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
An intersection is ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for pedestrians crossing
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: A steep grade is ahead. Check your brakes.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
A railroad crossing is ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for pedestrians crossing
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A series of curves ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
Never drive in this lane
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
An intersection is ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
An intersection is ahead
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Sharp right then left turns ahead
Never drive in this lane
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for pedestrians crossing
Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
A series of curves ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Road construction or detour ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What should you do at a steady red traffic light?
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Come to a complete stop and remain stopped
Stop only if other vehicles are present
Speed up to clear the intersection
questions.explanation: A steady red light means you must come to a full stop and wait until the light turns green and the way is clear.

questions.prompt: A steady yellow traffic light means:
choices: Speed up to beat the red light
The light is about to turn red, so stop if you can do so safely
Stop and wait for a green light
Yield to pedestrians only
questions.explanation: Yellow warns that red is coming next, so you should stop when it is safe rather than racing through.

questions.prompt: What does a flashing red traffic signal mean?
choices: Slow down and continue
Treat it like a stop sign: stop, then proceed when safe
The signal is broken; ignore it
Yield without stopping
questions.explanation: A flashing red light requires a complete stop, after which you may proceed when the way is clear, just like a stop sign.

questions.prompt: What shape is a stop sign?
choices: Triangle
Diamond
Octagon (eight sides)
Rectangle
questions.explanation: The eight-sided octagon is used only for stop signs so drivers can recognize it instantly, even from behind.

questions.prompt: A yellow diamond-shaped sign usually indicates:
choices: A regulatory speed limit
A warning about conditions or hazards ahead
A destination or guide
A construction zone end
questions.explanation: Yellow diamond signs are warning signs that alert you to hazards such as curves, intersections, or pedestrian crossings ahead.

questions.prompt: When a traffic signal is completely dark and not working, you should:
choices: Drive through without stopping
Treat the intersection as an all-way stop
Wait until the lights come back on
Always yield to traffic on the left
questions.explanation: A dark or non-functioning signal should be handled as a four-way stop, with each driver stopping and yielding in turn.

questions.prompt: A solid yellow line on your side of the center of a two-lane road means:
choices: Passing is allowed
Do not pass
The road is one-way
You may park here
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side of the road means passing is prohibited because it is unsafe to cross.

questions.prompt: What does a triangular sign that points downward mean?
choices: Stop
Yield
Do not enter
No passing
questions.explanation: The downward-pointing triangle is the yield sign, telling you to slow down and give the right of way to other traffic.

questions.prompt: A green arrow signal pointing left means:
choices: You may turn left and oncoming traffic is stopped
You must yield to oncoming traffic before turning
Left turns are prohibited
Stop before turning left
questions.explanation: A green arrow gives you a protected turn, meaning oncoming and conflicting traffic is being held by a red light.

questions.prompt: White lane lines on a roadway separate:
choices: Traffic moving in opposite directions
Traffic moving in the same direction
Parking spaces only
Bicycle lanes only
questions.explanation: White lines separate lanes of traffic flowing the same way, while yellow lines separate opposing traffic.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, if two vehicles arrive at the same time, who goes first?
choices: The vehicle on the left
The vehicle on the right
The faster vehicle
The larger vehicle
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach a four-way stop simultaneously, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When making a left turn, you must yield to:
choices: No one, since you have the right of way
Oncoming traffic and pedestrians in the crosswalk
Only vehicles behind you
Parked cars
questions.explanation: A left-turning driver must wait for oncoming vehicles to pass and for pedestrians to clear the crosswalk before turning.

questions.prompt: In Texas, you may turn right on a red light when:
choices: Never
After a complete stop, if no sign prohibits it and the way is clear
Only between sunrise and sunset
Only if you slow down without stopping
questions.explanation: Right turns on red are allowed after a full stop, provided no sign forbids it and you yield to pedestrians and cross traffic.

questions.prompt: When an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren on, you should:
choices: Speed up to get out of the way
Pull over to the right and stop
Stop immediately in your lane
Continue at the same speed
questions.explanation: Drivers must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling to the right edge of the road and stopping until they pass.

questions.prompt: Who has the right of way at an uncontrolled intersection where two vehicles arrive together?
choices: The vehicle on the left
The vehicle on the right
Whoever honks first
The vehicle going straight always
questions.explanation: Without signs or signals, the vehicle on the left yields to the one on the right when both arrive at the same time.

questions.prompt: When entering a roundabout, you must:
choices: Stop and let all traffic enter first
Yield to traffic already in the roundabout
Speed up to merge quickly
Always come to a complete stop
questions.explanation: Vehicles already circulating in a roundabout have the right of way, so you must yield before entering.

questions.prompt: A pedestrian is in a marked crosswalk as you approach. You should:
choices: Honk and continue
Stop and yield to the pedestrian
Drive around the pedestrian
Proceed if you have a green light
questions.explanation: Drivers must always yield to pedestrians in a crosswalk, even when the driver otherwise has the right of way.

questions.prompt: In Texas, a left turn on a red light is permitted only when:
choices: Turning from a one-way street onto another one-way street
There is no oncoming traffic
It is during daylight hours
You are turning into a driveway
questions.explanation: Texas allows a left turn on red only when moving from one one-way street onto another one-way street, after stopping.

questions.prompt: How far before a turn should you generally signal?
choices: At the moment you turn
At least 100 feet before turning
Only if other cars are present
10 feet before turning
questions.explanation: Signaling at least 100 feet ahead gives other drivers time to react to your intended turn.

questions.prompt: When you want to turn but a pedestrian is crossing where you intend to go, you should:
choices: Complete the turn quickly
Wait until the pedestrian has safely cleared your path
Edge forward to encourage them to hurry
Turn behind them while they cross
questions.explanation: You must let pedestrians finish crossing safely before completing your turn.

questions.prompt: In Texas, what is the default speed limit in an urban or residential district where no limit is posted?
choices: 20 mph
30 mph
45 mph
55 mph
questions.explanation: Unless otherwise posted, the prima facie speed limit in Texas urban and residential districts is 30 mph.

questions.prompt: What is the default speed limit on a numbered highway in a rural area of Texas where none is posted?
choices: 55 mph
60 mph
70 mph
80 mph
questions.explanation: The prima facie limit on numbered U.S. and state highways outside urban areas in Texas is 70 mph unless posted otherwise.

questions.prompt: The basic speed law requires drivers to:
choices: Always drive the posted speed limit
Drive at a speed that is reasonable and prudent for conditions
Drive as fast as traffic allows
Never exceed 55 mph
questions.explanation: Even at or below the posted limit, you must adjust your speed to conditions like weather, traffic, and visibility.

questions.prompt: A good rule for safe following distance in good conditions is:
choices: One car length
The two-second rule
The three-second rule
Ten feet per 10 mph
questions.explanation: The three-second rule gives you enough time and space to react to sudden stops by the vehicle ahead.

questions.prompt: You should increase your following distance when:
choices: The road is dry and clear
It is raining or visibility is poor
Traffic is light
You are in a hurry
questions.explanation: Poor weather lengthens stopping distance and reduces visibility, so you need extra space ahead.

questions.prompt: When driving in a school zone with the lights flashing, you should:
choices: Maintain highway speed
Slow to the posted school-zone speed and watch for children
Stop completely until the lights stop
Use your horn continuously
questions.explanation: Reduced school-zone limits protect children, and fines are often doubled for violations.

questions.prompt: Driving much slower than surrounding traffic is:
choices: Always the safest choice
Potentially dangerous because it disrupts traffic flow
Required for new drivers
Only a problem on highways
questions.explanation: Going too slowly can cause other drivers to brake or pass unexpectedly, increasing crash risk.

questions.prompt: When following a large truck, you should leave extra space because trucks:
choices: Are required to brake suddenly
Need more distance to stop and have larger blind spots
Always drive faster than cars
Cannot turn
questions.explanation: Large trucks take longer to stop and have wide blind spots, so extra following distance keeps you visible and safe.

questions.prompt: Stopping distance increases as your speed:
choices: Decreases
Stays the same
Increases
Has no effect on stopping
questions.explanation: Higher speeds require much greater distance to stop, since both reaction and braking distance grow with speed.

questions.prompt: On a multi-lane highway, the general rule for lane use is:
choices: Stay in the left lane at all times
Keep right except when passing
Drive in the center lane only
Change lanes frequently
questions.explanation: Keeping right except to pass lets faster traffic flow safely in the left lane.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should:
choices: Only check your rearview mirror
Signal, check mirrors, and look over your shoulder to clear the blind spot
Honk your horn
Speed up significantly
questions.explanation: Mirrors alone do not show the blind spot, so a shoulder check is needed before moving over.

questions.prompt: You should NOT pass another vehicle when:
choices: The road is straight and clear
Approaching a hill or curve where you cannot see ahead
Traffic is light
The vehicle ahead is slow
questions.explanation: Limited sight distance on hills and curves makes passing dangerous because oncoming traffic may be hidden.

questions.prompt: When a school bus stops with its red lights flashing and stop arm out on an undivided road, you must:
choices: Slow down and pass carefully
Stop, whether behind it or meeting it
Stop only if behind the bus
Honk and continue
questions.explanation: On an undivided road, traffic in both directions must stop for a school bus loading or unloading children.

questions.prompt: Under Texas's Move Over or Slow Down law, when approaching a stopped emergency or TxDOT vehicle with flashing lights, you must:
choices: Maintain your speed
Move over a lane or slow to at least 20 mph below the posted limit
Stop completely in your lane
Speed up to pass quickly
questions.explanation: Texas law requires drivers to change lanes away from the vehicle or slow to 20 mph below the limit (5 mph if the limit is 25 or less).

questions.prompt: After passing a vehicle, you should return to your lane when:
choices: Immediately after pulling alongside
You can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
The other driver waves you in
You reach the next exit
questions.explanation: Seeing the passed vehicle in your mirror confirms you have enough room to return safely without cutting it off.

questions.prompt: A broken yellow center line means:
choices: Never pass
Passing is allowed when it is safe
The road is one-way
Stop ahead
questions.explanation: A broken yellow line indicates passing is permitted on your side when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: Slower-moving traffic on a multi-lane road should:
choices: Stay in the left lane
Move to the right lane
Drive in any lane
Stop frequently
questions.explanation: Keeping slower vehicles to the right allows faster traffic to pass safely on the left.

questions.prompt: It is illegal to pass on a two-lane road within how many feet of an intersection or railroad crossing?
choices: 50 feet
100 feet
300 feet
Any distance is fine
questions.explanation: Passing is prohibited within 100 feet of an intersection or railroad crossing because of the danger of crossing traffic.

questions.prompt: Because motorcycles are smaller and harder to see, drivers should:
choices: Share a lane side by side with them
Check blind spots and give them a full lane width
Follow closely behind them
Pass them without signaling
questions.explanation: Motorcycles are entitled to a full lane, and careful blind-spot checks help you see them before changing lanes.

questions.prompt: When you see a pedestrian using a white cane or guide dog, you must:
choices: Honk to alert them
Yield the right of way
Drive around quickly
Expect them to yield to you
questions.explanation: A white cane or guide dog signals a person who is blind, and drivers must always yield to them.

questions.prompt: The large areas around a truck where the driver cannot see other vehicles are called:
choices: Crumple zones
No-zones or blind spots
Safety lanes
Buffer zones
questions.explanation: Trucks have large blind spots, or no-zones, where your vehicle may be invisible to the driver.

questions.prompt: When sharing the road with a bicyclist, you should:
choices: Pass closely to save time
Pass at a safe distance and only when it is clear
Expect them to ride on the sidewalk
Honk to make them move
questions.explanation: Bicyclists have a legal right to the road, so give them ample space when passing.

questions.prompt: In areas where children may be present, such as near schools and parks, you should:
choices: Maintain your normal speed
Slow down and stay alert for children entering the street
Use your high beams
Pass other cars quickly
questions.explanation: Children can be unpredictable, so reduce speed and watch carefully near schools, parks, and neighborhoods.

questions.prompt: You must remain stopped for a school bus with flashing red lights until:
choices: You feel it is safe to go
The red lights stop flashing and the bus begins to move
Five seconds have passed
The bus driver honks
questions.explanation: Stay stopped until the bus's red lights turn off and it starts moving, ensuring all children are clear.

questions.prompt: If you are driving slowly and a line of vehicles forms behind you, you should:
choices: Speed up beyond the limit
Pull over when safe to let them pass
Brake to slow them down
Ignore them
questions.explanation: Letting faster traffic pass when safe reduces frustration and the risk of unsafe passing maneuvers.

questions.prompt: Motorcyclists may ride within a single lane and:
choices: Should be crowded out by cars
Are entitled to the full width of a lane
Must always ride on the shoulder
May be passed within the same lane by cars
questions.explanation: A motorcycle has the right to use a full traffic lane, and cars should not try to share it.

questions.prompt: What is the legal BAC limit for drivers 21 and older in Texas?
questions.explanation: Drivers 21 and older are legally intoxicated at a blood alcohol concentration of 0.08% or higher.

questions.prompt: For drivers under 21 in Texas, the amount of alcohol allowed in their system while driving is:
choices: Up to 0.08%
Up to 0.05%
Any detectable amount is illegal (zero tolerance)
Up to 0.02%
questions.explanation: Texas has a zero-tolerance law, so any detectable alcohol in a driver under 21 is illegal.

questions.prompt: Texas law prohibits which activity for all drivers?
choices: Using cruise control
Sending or reading text messages while driving
Listening to the radio
Drinking water
questions.explanation: Texting while driving is banned statewide because it dangerously distracts the driver.

questions.prompt: A driver with a Texas learner license may use a cell phone while driving:
choices: Anytime hands-free
Only to send texts
Not at all, except in an emergency
Only at stoplights
questions.explanation: Learner license holders and all drivers under 18 are banned from using any wireless device except in emergencies.

questions.prompt: The best way to reduce a high blood alcohol concentration is:
choices: Drinking coffee
Taking a cold shower
Time
Eating a large meal
questions.explanation: Only time allows the body to eliminate alcohol; coffee, food, and showers do not speed it up.

questions.prompt: By driving on Texas roads, you give implied consent to:
choices: Vehicle inspections at any time
Chemical testing for alcohol or drugs if lawfully arrested
Random searches of your home
Paying higher taxes
questions.explanation: Implied consent means refusing a breath or blood test after a lawful DWI arrest leads to automatic license suspension.

questions.prompt: Which of the following can impair your ability to drive safely?
choices: Only illegal drugs
Only alcohol
Alcohol, illegal drugs, and some prescription or over-the-counter medicines
Nothing if you feel fine
questions.explanation: Many legal medications cause drowsiness or slowed reactions, impairing driving just as alcohol and illegal drugs do.

questions.prompt: Alcohol affects driving by:
choices: Improving reaction time
Slowing reaction time and impairing judgment
Sharpening vision
Having no real effect in small amounts
questions.explanation: Even small amounts of alcohol slow reactions and impair the judgment needed to drive safely.

questions.prompt: If you are too tired to drive safely, the best action is to:
choices: Open a window and keep going
Turn up the radio
Stop and rest or let someone else drive
Drive faster to arrive sooner
questions.explanation: Drowsy driving is as dangerous as impaired driving, so the only real fix is to rest.

questions.prompt: When driving in fog, you should use:
choices: High-beam headlights
Low-beam headlights
No headlights
Hazard lights while moving
questions.explanation: High beams reflect off fog and reduce visibility, so low beams provide better sight in fog.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to hydroplane, you should:
choices: Brake hard immediately
Ease off the accelerator and keep the wheel straight
Turn the wheel sharply
Speed up to regain control
questions.explanation: Easing off the gas lets the tires regain contact with the road, while braking or turning hard can cause a skid.

questions.prompt: Roads are often most slippery:
choices: After it has rained for several hours
During the first rain after a dry spell
Only when it snows
On sunny days
questions.explanation: Oil and dust rise to the surface when rain first falls, making the road especially slick before it washes away.

questions.prompt: Texas law requires headlights to be on:
choices: Only during heavy rain
From 30 minutes after sunset to 30 minutes before sunrise
Only on highways
Only when other cars approach
questions.explanation: Headlights are required during nighttime hours and whenever conditions limit visibility.

questions.prompt: If your car starts to skid, you should:
choices: Slam on the brakes
Steer in the direction you want the front of the car to go
Take your hands off the wheel
Accelerate hard
questions.explanation: Gentle steering in your intended direction, without hard braking, helps you regain control of a skid.

questions.prompt: In rain, snow, or reduced visibility, you should:
choices: Drive faster to get through it
Reduce speed and increase following distance
Use high beams in fog
Follow closely to see better
questions.explanation: Slowing down and leaving more space compensates for reduced traction and longer stopping distances.

questions.prompt: Many drivers turn on their headlights whenever:
choices: The radio is on
Their windshield wipers are in use
They are parked
Traffic is heavy
questions.explanation: Using headlights whenever wipers are running improves your visibility and helps others see you in bad weather.

questions.prompt: When approaching a flooded roadway, you should:
choices: Drive through quickly
Turn around and find another route
Stop in the water and wait
Follow the car ahead closely
questions.explanation: Even shallow moving water can sweep a vehicle away, so it is safest to turn around rather than drive through.

questions.prompt: In Texas, you may not park within how many feet of a fire hydrant?
choices: 5 feet
10 feet
15 feet
25 feet
questions.explanation: Parking within 15 feet of a fire hydrant is prohibited so firefighters can reach it in an emergency.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, turn your front wheels:
choices: Toward the curb
Away from the curb
Straight ahead
It does not matter
questions.explanation: Turning the wheels toward the curb when facing downhill lets the curb stop the car if it rolls.

questions.prompt: When parking facing uphill with a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
choices: Toward the curb
Away from the curb
Straight
Either direction
questions.explanation: Facing uphill, turning the wheels away from the curb lets the tire roll back into the curb and stop the car.

questions.prompt: It is illegal to park:
choices: In a marked parking space
In a crosswalk or on a sidewalk
In your own driveway
In a parking garage
questions.explanation: Parking in a crosswalk or on a sidewalk blocks pedestrians and is prohibited.

questions.prompt: Before pulling away from a parallel parking spot at the curb, you should:
choices: Pull out quickly
Signal and yield to traffic already in the lane
Honk your horn
Reverse first
questions.explanation: You must signal and yield to oncoming traffic before merging back into the travel lane.

questions.prompt: You may park in a space reserved for people with disabilities:
choices: Anytime for a few minutes
Only with a valid disabled placard or plate
If no other spaces are available
During business hours only
questions.explanation: Accessible parking spaces require a valid disabled placard or license plate, regardless of how briefly you stop.

questions.prompt: When you leave your parked vehicle, you should:
choices: Leave the engine running
Turn off the engine, remove the key, and set the parking brake
Leave the doors unlocked
Leave it in neutral on a hill
questions.explanation: Securing the vehicle prevents it from rolling or being stolen while unattended.

questions.prompt: When parking on a hill where there is no curb, turn your wheels:
choices: Toward the center of the road
Toward the edge of the road
Straight ahead
It does not matter
questions.explanation: With no curb, turning the wheels toward the road's edge keeps a runaway vehicle from rolling into traffic.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum age to apply for a learner license in Texas?
questions.explanation: Texas allows teens to apply for a learner license at age 15 after enrolling in driver education.

questions.prompt: While holding a Texas learner license, you must be accompanied by:
choices: Any licensed driver
A licensed driver at least 21 years old in the front passenger seat
A parent only
No one if you drive carefully
questions.explanation: A learner license requires a supervising driver who is 21 or older seated in the front passenger seat at all times.

questions.prompt: Texas teen drivers must complete how many hours of supervised behind-the-wheel practice?
choices: 10 hours
20 hours
30 hours, including 10 at night
50 hours, including 20 at night
questions.explanation: Teen applicants must log 30 hours of supervised driving, at least 10 of which are at night.

questions.prompt: A Texas provisional license holder under 18 may not drive between:
choices: 10 p.m. and 6 a.m.
Midnight and 5 a.m., except for work, school, or emergencies
9 p.m. and 5 a.m.
11 p.m. and 4 a.m.
questions.explanation: Provisional drivers under 18 face a nighttime curfew from midnight to 5 a.m. with limited exceptions.

questions.prompt: A Texas provisional license holder under 18 may carry how many passengers under 21 who are not family?
choices: No more than one
No more than two
As many as fit
No limit
questions.explanation: Provisional drivers may carry only one non-family passenger under 21 to reduce distractions.

questions.prompt: After holding a learner license, how long must a teen wait before applying for a provisional license?
choices: No waiting period
At least six months
At least one year
At least two years
questions.explanation: A teen must hold the learner license for at least six months and be 16 before getting a provisional license.

questions.prompt: The nighttime and passenger restrictions on a Texas provisional license end:
choices: When the driver turns 16
After the license has been held for 12 months
Immediately
Only at age 21
questions.explanation: The curfew and passenger limits automatically lift once the provisional license has been held for 12 months.

questions.prompt: You must carry which documents while driving in Texas?
choices: Only your driver license
Your driver license and proof of insurance
Only your vehicle registration
A copy of the driver handbook
questions.explanation: Drivers must carry and show their license and proof of financial responsibility (insurance) when requested.

questions.prompt: Who must wear a seat belt in a vehicle in Texas?
choices: Only the driver
Only front-seat occupants
Every occupant of the vehicle
Only children
questions.explanation: Texas requires all occupants, regardless of seating position, to be buckled up.

questions.prompt: In Texas, a child must ride in a child safety seat until they are at least:
choices: 4 years old
8 years old or taller than 4 feet 9 inches
10 years old
6 years old
questions.explanation: Children under 8 must use a child safety seat unless they are taller than 4 feet 9 inches.

questions.prompt: If you are involved in a crash, you are legally required to:
choices: Leave if no police are present
Stop and provide information, and help if anyone is injured
Drive to the nearest police station only
Move your car and continue driving
questions.explanation: Leaving the scene of a crash without stopping and exchanging information is a serious offense.

questions.prompt: Driving without the required auto insurance in Texas is:
choices: Allowed for new drivers
A violation of the financial responsibility law
Only a problem after a crash
Permitted with a learner license
questions.explanation: Texas requires every driver to maintain liability insurance, and driving without it is illegal.

questions.prompt: When stopped by a police officer, you should:
choices: Get out of the car quickly
Pull over safely, stay in your vehicle, and keep your hands visible
Drive to a parking lot far away
Argue about the stop
questions.explanation: Pulling over promptly and keeping your hands visible keeps both you and the officer safe.

questions.prompt: The driver is responsible for ensuring that passengers under which age are properly restrained?
choices: Under 10
Under 17
Under 21
Only the driver's own children
questions.explanation: In Texas the driver is legally responsible for making sure all passengers under 17 are properly buckled.

questions.prompt: Refusing to take a breath or blood test after a lawful DWI arrest in Texas results in:
choices: No consequences
An automatic driver license suspension
A warning only
A reduced fine
questions.explanation: Under implied consent, refusing a chemical test leads to an automatic license suspension.

questions.prompt: Accumulating many traffic convictions can lead to:
choices: A free driving course
Higher insurance costs and possible license suspension
Automatic license renewal
No effect on your record
questions.explanation: Repeated violations raise insurance rates and can cause your driving privileges to be suspended.
